title: cynoglossum lanceolatum subsp. eulanceolatum
intro: Cynoglossum lanceolatum subsp. eulanceolatum, also known as the Hound's Tongue, is a plant species within the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to Western North America. The plant's roots have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties of antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Cynoglossum lanceolatum subsp. eulanceolatum include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve infusions, tinctures, or decoctions of the roots, which can be used externally to treat wounds and skin conditions, or taken internally to address digestive and inflammatory issues.
